calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-07-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Ruth Burke appeared by zoom for and with Elizabeth Soloway by zoom.
Frank Cialone appeared by zoom for Kevin Littlejohn, Keith Littlejohn and Rosemarie Dito (Heirs).
Thomas Cain appeared by zoom for American Research Bureau (partial Assignee).

The Parties addressed the issue of the appraisal fee for the Vacaville property, the services that were provided by Meg Connell, the condition of the home (toxic moldy materials), the time spent by Ms. Connell for finding items that were requested by the Heirs and getting the property in order so that the Heirs could come through and identify what they want, whether or not some portion of the work performed by Ms. Connell would be covered in the ordinary fees, time spent by Ms. Soloway at the home looking for paper work regarding assets and taxes as well as walking the Heirs through the home and making sure everyone got what they wanted, etc. Further discussion was held regarding the issue of whether or not the extraordinary fees are reasonable given the volume of work, the petition for probate being contested, the need to ascertain who the proper heirs were and whether 15 hours of time for the petition for the petition for probate was reasonable, etc. Mr. Cain explained services provided by American Research, why they do not do time sheets, the process American Research takes in identifying an estate and the genealogical research done to determine if it is reasonable to find heirs, the risk and liability of American Research, and addresses the issue of whether or not the percentages fees charged by American Research are grossly unreasonable. Upon conclusion, the Petition for Settlement of First and Final Account, Final Distribution, Payment of Statutory Fees and Fees for Extraordinary Services is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate.
Off Calendar Sep-09-2020 Continued To Oct-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ruth Burke appeared by zoom for Elizabeth Soloway.
Douglas Applegate appeared by zoom for Kemp & Associates (Interested Party).

The Court questions why no petition for final distribution, or petition for approval of report on status of administration was filed. Ms. Burke stated that they are in the process of final distribution, that they had to sell two pieces of property and that there was a delay due to COVID. Ms. Burke also stated that the San Francisco property closed on August 26th and that she hopes to have the petition filed within 15 to 30-days. The Court questions if the bond has been increased and whether the sale proceeds are in a blocked account. Ms. Burke stated that she does not believe they have increased bond, that the sale proceeds are not in a blocked account and that the funds are in the estate account. The Court instructed Ms. Burke to file an ex parte petition to increase bond as soon as possible and informed Ms. Burke that her e-mail bounced back. Ms. Burke stated that she got an e-mail from the Court two or three weeks ago, that her correct e-mail is ruth@kollerburkelaw.com, that the accounting is almost done and that she thinks she can get it filed within three weeks. Status on close of estate is continued to October 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition is not ready to be filed, Ms. Burke will need to notify the Court one week before the hearing date. Status on filing of an ex parte petition to increase bond is also set for October 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Jun-12-2019 Continued To Jul-10-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ruth Burke for Elizabeth Soloway (Administrator).
Frank Cialone for Keith Littlejohn, Kevin Littlejohn and Rose Dito (Heirs).
Douglas Applegate for Kemp & Associates (Interested Party).

Mr. Applegate stated that Attorney Thomas Caine is requesting a continuance. Ms. Burke stated that she provided information to Mr. McSweeney who represents Genevieve and Carl (Heirs), and that based on what she provided Mr. McSweeney stated that he withdraws the opposition. Mr. Cialone stated that he believes the petition for heirship should be granted, that the response to the opposition which he filed yesterday wraps things up as far as his Clients, and spoke of the heirs and how they are related. The Petition to Determine Heirship is continued to July 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. McSweeney to withdraw his objection.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
